{{神社| 名称=徳山大神宮| 所在地=北海道松前郡松前町字神明66| 祭神=天照大御神
豊受大神| 社格=郷社| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[9月17日]]| 神事=松前神楽
鎮釜湯立神事}} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島支庁|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を建て、「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治8年12月に徳山大神宮に改称し、明治9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を主[[祭神]]とする。近隣の47社の[[神社合祀|合祀]]により、以下の柱の46神を併せ祀る（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁財天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神|上筒男命]]（住吉神） *中筒男命（住吉神） *底筒男命（住吉神） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *級長津姫神 *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナギ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] [[Category:北海道の神社|とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:郷社|とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:一宮|とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:神明神社|とくやまたいしんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=徳山大神宮| 所在地=北海道松前郡松前町字神明66| 祭神=天照大御神
豊受大神| 社格=郷社| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[9月17日]]| 神事=松前神楽
鎮釜湯立神事}} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島支庁|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を建て、「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治8年12月に徳山大神宮に改称し、明治9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を主[[祭神]]とする。近隣の47社の[[神社合祀|合祀]]により、以下の柱の46神を併せ祀る（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁財天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神|上筒男命]]（住吉神） *中筒男命（住吉神） *底筒男命（住吉神） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *級長津姫神 *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナギ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] [[Category:松前町 (北海道)|とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:北海道の神社|とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:郷社|とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:一宮|とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:神明神社|とくやまたいしんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=徳山大神宮| 所在地=北海道松前郡松前町字神明66| 祭神=天照大御神
豊受大神| 社格=郷社| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[9月17日]]| 神事=松前神楽
鎮釜湯立神事}} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島支庁|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を建て、「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治8年12月に徳山大神宮に改称し、明治9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を主[[祭神]]とする。近隣の47社の[[神社合祀|合祀]]により、以下の柱の46神を併せ祀る（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁才天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神|上筒男命]]（住吉神） *中筒男命（住吉神） *底筒男命（住吉神） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *級長津姫神 *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナキ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とくやまたいしんくう}} [[Category:渡島国]] [[Category:松前町 (北海道)]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:神明神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=徳山大神宮| 所在地=北海道松前郡松前町字神明66| 祭神=天照大御神
豊受大神| 社格=郷社| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[9月17日]]| 神事=松前神楽
鎮釜湯立神事}} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島国|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を建て、「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治8年12月に徳山大神宮に改称し、明治9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を主[[祭神]]とする。近隣の47社の[[神社合祀|合祀]]により、以下の柱の46神を併せ祀る（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁才天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神|上筒男命]]（住吉神） *中筒男命（住吉神） *底筒男命（住吉神） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *級長津姫神 *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナキ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とくやまたいしんくう}} [[Category:渡島国]] [[Category:松前町 (北海道)]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:神明神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=徳山大神宮| 所在地=北海道松前郡松前町字神明66| 祭神=天照大御神
豊受大神| 社格=郷社| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[9月17日]]| 神事=松前神楽
鎮釜湯立神事}} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島国|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を建て、「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治8年12月に徳山大神宮に改称し、明治9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を主[[祭神]]とする。近隣の47社の[[神社合祀|合祀]]により、以下の柱の46神を併せ祀る（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麻呂|柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁才天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神|上筒男命]]（住吉神） *中筒男命（住吉神） *底筒男命（住吉神） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *級長津姫神 *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナギ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とくやまたいしんくう}} [[Category:渡島国|社とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:松前町 (北海道)]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:神明神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=徳山大神宮| 所在地=北海道松前郡松前町字神明66| 位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|41|26|05|N|140|06|38|E|scale:25000}}| 祭神=天照大御神
豊受大神| 社格=旧郷社| 創建=| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[9月17日]]| 神事=松前神楽
鎮釜湯立神事}} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島国|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を[[祭神|主祭神]]とするが、その他46柱の神々を[[合祀]]している（後述）。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を[[唐津内町]]に建て、これを「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治4年11月[[村社]]に列し、同8年12月に現社名「徳山大神宮」に改称、翌9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 昭和46年3月5日、本殿が北海道有形文化財に指定され、同52年に修理したが、これに併せて拝殿を神明造に改築、翌53年に竣工した。 == 境内社 == 羽黒神社（[[木花咲耶姫命]]) == 文化財 == *本殿（道指定） *棟札（道指定）（36） *鳥居（道指定） == 合祀神 == 近隣47神社を合祀したため、以下の46神を併せ祀っている（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麻呂|柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁才天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神]]（上筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津姫神]] *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナギ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] === 合祀神社一覧 === 古館稲荷神社・天満神社・人麿神社・奇音神社・北ノ丸稲荷神社・小松前稲荷神社・豊照神社・久須野神社・諏訪神社・北ノ丸天満宮・八幡宮・金刀比羅神社・全生室神社・磐舩神社・匠工祖神社・水神神社・三彦神社・天満神社・祓岩稲荷神社・住吉神社・天満神社・八郎神社・風神神社・琴比羅神社・天星宮稲荷神社・稲荷神社・千軒神社・狩場神社・利尻山神社・厳島神社・風神神社・日枝神社・廣峯神社・北野神社・船玉神社・稲荷神社・天満神社・稲荷神社・稲荷神社・二柱神社・妙見神社・馬形神社・徳山稲荷神社・天満神社・榊神社・琴比羅神社・春日神社 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 徳山大神宮]（北海道神社庁） {{DEFAULTSORT:とくやまたいしんくう}} [[Category:渡島国|社とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:松前町 (北海道)]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:神明神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

鎮釜湯立神事}} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島国|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を[[祭神|主祭神]]とするが、その他46柱の神々を[[合祀]]している（後述）。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を[[唐津内町]]に建て、これを「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治4年11月[[村社]]に列し、同8年12月に現社名「徳山大神宮」に改称、翌9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 昭和46年3月5日、本殿が北海道有形文化財に指定され、同52年に修理したが、これに併せて拝殿を神明造に改築、翌53年に竣工した。 == 境内社 == 羽黒神社（[[木花咲耶姫命]]) == 文化財 == *本殿（道指定） *棟札（道指定）（36） *鳥居（道指定） == 合祀神 == 近隣47神社を合祀したため、以下の46神を併せ祀っている（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麻呂|柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁才天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神]]（上筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津姫神]] *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナギ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] === 合祀神社一覧 === 古館稲荷神社・天満神社・人麿神社・奇音神社・北ノ丸稲荷神社・小松前稲荷神社・豊照神社・久須野神社・諏訪神社・北ノ丸天満宮・八幡宮・金刀比羅神社・全生室神社・磐舩神社・匠工祖神社・水神神社・三彦神社・天満神社・祓岩稲荷神社・住吉神社・天満神社・八郎神社・風神神社・琴比羅神社・天星宮稲荷神社・稲荷神社・千軒神社・狩場神社・利尻山神社・厳島神社・風神神社・日枝神社・廣峯神社・北野神社・船玉神社・稲荷神社・天満神社・稲荷神社・稲荷神社・二柱神社・妙見神社・馬形神社・徳山稲荷神社・天満神社・榊神社・琴比羅神社・春日神社 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 徳山大神宮]（北海道神社庁） {{DEFAULTSORT:とくやまたいしんくう}} [[Category:渡島国|社とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:松前町 (北海道)]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]] [[Category:神明神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

鎮釜湯立神事}} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島国|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を[[祭神|主祭神]]とするが、その他46柱の神々を[[合祀]]している（後述）。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を[[唐津内町]]に建て、これを「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治4年11月[[村社]]に列し、同8年12月に現社名「徳山大神宮」に改称、翌9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 昭和46年3月5日、本殿が北海道有形文化財に指定され、同52年に修理したが、これに併せて拝殿を神明造に改築、翌53年に竣工した。 == 境内社 == 羽黒神社（[[木花咲耶姫命]]) == 文化財 == *本殿（道指定） *棟札（道指定）（36） *鳥居（道指定） == 合祀神 == 近隣47神社を合祀したため、以下の46神を併せ祀っている（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麻呂|柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁才天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神]]（上筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津姫神]] *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナギ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] === 合祀神社一覧 === 古館稲荷神社・天満神社・人麿神社・奇音神社・北ノ丸稲荷神社・小松前稲荷神社・豊照神社・久須野神社・諏訪神社・北ノ丸天満宮・八幡宮・金刀比羅神社・全生室神社・磐舩神社・匠工祖神社・水神神社・三彦神社・天満神社・祓岩稲荷神社・住吉神社・天満神社・八郎神社・風神神社・琴比羅神社・天星宮稲荷神社・稲荷神社・千軒神社・狩場神社・利尻山神社・厳島神社・風神神社・日枝神社・廣峯神社・北野神社・船玉神社・稲荷神社・天満神社・稲荷神社・稲荷神社・二柱神社・妙見神社・馬形神社・徳山稲荷神社・天満神社・榊神社・琴比羅神社・春日神社 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 徳山大神宮]（北海道神社庁） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とくやまたいしんくう}} [[Category:渡島国|社とくやまたいしんくう]] [[Category:北海道松前町の歴史]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]] [[Category:神明神社]]

昭和46年3月5日、本殿が北海道有形文化財に指定され、同52年に修理したが、これに併せて拝殿を神明造に改築、翌53年に竣工した。 == 境内社 == 羽黒神社（[[木花咲耶姫命]]) == 文化財 == *本殿（道指定） *棟札（道指定）（36） *鳥居（道指定） == 合祀神 == 昭和46年3月5日、本殿が北海道有形文化財に指定され、同52年に修理したが、これに併せて拝殿を神明造に改築、翌53年に竣工した。 == 境内社 == 羽黒神社（[[木花咲耶姫命]]) == 文化財 == *本殿（道指定） *棟札（道指定）（36） *鳥居（道指定） == 合祀神 == 近隣47神社を合祀したため、以下の46神を併せ祀っている（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 {{col| *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麻呂|柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁才天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] | *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神]]（上筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津姫神]] *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナギ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] }} === 合祀神社一覧 === {{col| * 古館稲荷神社 * 天満神社 * 人麿神社 * 奇音神社 * 北ノ丸稲荷神社 * 小松前稲荷神社 * 豊照神社 * 久須野神社 * 諏訪神社 * 北ノ丸天満宮 | * 八幡宮 * 金刀比羅神社 * 全生室神社 * 磐舩神社 * 匠工祖神社 * 水神神社 * 三彦神社 * 天満神社 * 祓岩稲荷神社 * 住吉神社 | * 天満神社 * 八郎神社 * 風神神社 * 琴比羅神社 * 天星宮稲荷神社 * 鎮釜湯立神事}} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島国|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を[[祭神|主祭神]]とするが、その他46柱の神々を[[合祀]]している（後述）。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を[[唐津内町]]に建て、これを「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治4年11月[[村社]]に列し、同8年12月に現社名「徳山大神宮」に改称、翌9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 昭和46年3月5日、本殿が北海道有形文化財に指定され、同52年に修理したが、これに併せて拝殿を神明造に改築、翌53年に竣工した。 == 境内社 == 羽黒神社（[[木花咲耶姫命]]) == 文化財 == *本殿（道指定） *棟札（道指定）（36） *鳥居（道指定） == 合祀神 == 近隣47神社を合祀したため、以下の46神を併せ祀っている（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 {{col| *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麻呂|柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁才天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] | *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神]]（上筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津姫神]] *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナギ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] }} === 合祀神社一覧 === {{col| 大神宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''徳山大神宮'''（とくやまだいじんぐう）は、[[北海道]][[松前郡]][[松前町 (北海道)|松前町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[渡島国|渡島]][[一宮]]と称される。 ==祭神== [[天照大御神]]・[[豊受大神]]を[[祭神|主祭神]]とするが、その他46柱の神々を[[合祀]]している（後述）。 ==歴史== 創建の年代は不詳であるが、かつて、秋田・津軽の漁民は毎年春になると当地に渡って漁をし、秋に帰っていたが、[[アイヌ]]からの危難を逃れるために[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神札|大麻]]（お札）を祀る小祠を[[唐津内町]]に建て、これを「伊勢堂」と称したのに始まると伝えられる。 [[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）、領主[[蛎崎季広|蛎崎（松前）季広]]が蔵町（現在の松前町字福山）に遷座して「神明社」と改称し崇敬した。[[承応]]元年、[[松前藩]]主[[松前高広]]が現在地に社殿を造営して遷座し、伊勢神宮から正式に神璽を奉請した。明治4年11月[[村社]]に列し、同8年12月に現社名「徳山大神宮」に改称、翌9年10月、郷社に昇格した。大正4年2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 昭和46年3月5日、本殿が北海道有形文化財に指定され、同52年に修理したが、これに併せて拝殿を神明造に改築、翌53年に竣工した。 == 境内社 == 羽黒神社（[[木花咲耶姫命]]) == 文化財 == *本殿（道指定） *棟札（道指定）（36） *鳥居（道指定） == 合祀神 == 近隣47神社を合祀したため、以下の46神を併せ祀っている（同一の神を祀る神社が複数あったため、同じ神が複数柱祀られている）。 {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| *[[応神天皇|譽田別命]]（[[八幡神]]） *[[天児屋根命]]（[[春日神]]） *宇賀之賣命（[[稲荷神]]） *[[建御名方神|武御名方神]] *[[国魂|大國魂命]] *[[大国主|大名持神]] *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]（稲荷神） *[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]] *[[大宮能売神|大宮女神]] *[[菅原道真]]（[[天神信仰|天神]]） *[[柿本人麻呂|柿本人麿]] *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[弁才天]]） *菅原道眞（天神） *菅原道眞（天神） *武彦命 *醫彦命 *興彦命 *[[ミズハノメ|水波廼女神]] *[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]] *[[手置帆負命]] *[[彦狭知命]] *倉稲魂命（稲荷神） *[[住吉三神]]（上筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命） *菅原道真（天神） *[[源為朝]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦神]] *[[シナツヒコ|級長津姫神]] *[[大物主|大物主神]] *[[崇徳天皇]] *倉稲魂神（稲荷神） *宇迦之御魂命（稲荷神） *[[大山咋神]] *天之御柱神（[[龍田大社|龍田神]]） *國之御柱神（龍田神） *[[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] *[[タカミムスビ|髙皇産霊神]] *[[カミムスビ|神皇彦霊神]] *[[イザナギ|伊弉諾神]] *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊神]] *松前平治衛門廣峯ノ霊 *[[カグツチ|火産霊神]] *[[経津主神|経津主命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] }} === 合祀神社一覧 === {{columns-list|colwidth=10em| * 古館稲荷神社 * 天満神社 * 人麿神社 * 奇音神社 * 北ノ丸稲荷神社 * 小松前稲荷神社 * 豊照神社 * 久須野神社 * 諏訪神社 * 北ノ丸天満宮 * 八幡宮 * 金刀比羅神社 * 全生室神社 * 磐舩神社 * 匠工祖神社 * 水神神社 * 三彦神社 * 天満神社 * 祓岩稲荷神社 * 住吉神社 * 天満神社 * 八郎神社 * 風神神社 * 琴比羅神社 * 天星宮稲荷神社 * 稲荷神社 * 千軒神社 * 狩場神社 * 利尻山神社 * 厳島神社 * 風神神社 * 日枝神社 * 廣峯神社 * 北野神社 * 船玉神社 * 稲荷神社 * 天満神社 * 稲荷神社 * 稲荷神社 * 二柱神社 * 妙見神社 * 馬形神社 * 徳山稲荷神社 * 天満神社 * 榊神社 * 琴比羅神社 * 春日神社 }} == 脚注 == {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zō]] | 例祭 = [[:en:September 17|September 17]] | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|qid=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|qq=|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|qq=|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|qq=|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|qq=|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|qq=|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|qq=|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|qq=|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|qq=|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|qq=|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zō]] | 例祭 = [[:en:September 17|September 17]] | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]]) * {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zō]] | 例祭 = [[:en:September 17|September 17]] | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]]) * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (【associated with】 {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]][[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the
