{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zō]] | 例祭 = [[:en:September 17|September 17]] | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Matsumae District, Hokkaido|Matsumae District]], {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]], they built a small shrine enshrining the [[:en:Cannabis (drug)|Osema]] (talisman) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1582|1582]]), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Shōō]] the lord of the [[:en:Matsumae Domain|Matsumae Domain]], {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. In the 4th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]], the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zō}} | 例祭 = {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|WD=Q2894|lt=September 17}} | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}}, {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu}}, they built a small shrine enshrining the {{ill|Cannabis (drug)|en|Cannabis (drug)|WD=Q2845|lt=Osema}} (talisman) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1582|en|1582|WD=Q6719|lt=1582}}), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Shōō}} the lord of the {{ill|Matsumae Domain|en|Matsumae Domain|WD=Q18669712|lt=Matsumae Domain}}, {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}}. In the 4th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}, the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * [[ja:徳山大神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11489606}} [[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} [[Category:Buildings in Matsumae Town, Hokkaido]] {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zō}} | 例祭 = {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|WD=Q2894|lt=September 17}} | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}}, {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu}}, they built a small shrine enshrining the {{ill|Cannabis (drug)|en|Cannabis (drug)|WD=Q2845|lt=Osema}} (talisman) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1582|en|1582|WD=Q6719|lt=1582}}), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Shōō}} the lord of the {{ill|Matsumae Domain|en|Matsumae Domain|WD=Q18669712|lt=Matsumae Domain}}, {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}}. In the 4th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}, the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * [[ja:徳山大神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11489606}} [[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} [[Category:Buildings in Matsumae Town, Hokkaido]] [[ja:徳山大神宮]] [[ja:徳山大神宮]] [[ja:徳山大神宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11489606]] Wikidata name: Tokuyama Daijingū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2016年5月31日 (火) 13:15 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | 名称 = {{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}} | 画像 = {{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|41|26|05|N|140|06|38|E}}}} | 所在地 = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}} {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}} {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} 字{{ill|Shinmei|ja|神明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei|1=Shinmei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590276}}66 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|41|26|05|N|140|06|38|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神 = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zō}} | 例祭 = {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|WD=Q2894|lt=September 17}} | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}}, {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu}}, they built a small shrine enshrining the {{ill|Cannabis (drug)|en|Cannabis (drug)|WD=Q2845|lt=Osema}} (talisman) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1582|en|1582|WD=Q6719|lt=1582}}), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Shōō}} the lord of the {{ill|Matsumae Domain|en|Matsumae Domain|WD=Q18669712|lt=Matsumae Domain}}, {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}}. In the 4th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}, the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * [[ja:徳山大神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11489606}} [[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} [[Category:Buildings in Matsumae Town, Hokkaido]] 拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|41|26|05|N|140|06|38|E}}}} | 所在地 = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}} {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}} {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} 字{{ill|Shinmei|ja|神明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei|1=Shinmei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590276}}66 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|41|26|05|N|140|06|38|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神 = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zō}} | 例祭 = {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|WD=Q2894|lt=September 17}} | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}}, {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu}}, they built a small shrine enshrining the {{ill|Cannabis (drug)|en|Cannabis (drug)|WD=Q2845|lt=Osema}} (talisman) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1582|en|1582|WD=Q6719|lt=1582}}), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Shōō}} the lord of the {{ill|Matsumae Domain|en|Matsumae Domain|WD=Q18669712|lt=Matsumae Domain}}, {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}}. In the 4th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}, the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (【associated with】 [[ja:徳山大神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11489606}} [[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} [[Category:Buildings in Matsumae Town, Hokkaido]] {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zō}} | 例祭 = {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|WD=Q2894|lt=September 17}} | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}}, {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu}}, they built a small shrine enshrining the {{ill|Cannabis (drug)|en|Cannabis (drug)|WD=Q2845|lt=Osema}} (talisman) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1582|en|1582|WD=Q6719|lt=1582}}), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Shōō}} the lord of the {{ill|Matsumae Domain|en|Matsumae Domain|WD=Q18669712|lt=Matsumae Domain}}, {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}}. In the 4th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}, the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * [[ja:徳山大神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11489606}} [[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} [[Category:Buildings in Matsumae Town, Hokkaido]] {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}}, {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu}}, they built a small shrine enshrining the {{ill|Cannabis (drug)|en|Cannabis (drug)|WD=Q2845|lt=Osema}} (talisman) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1582|en|1582|WD=Q6719|lt=1582}}), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Shōō}} the lord of the {{ill|Matsumae Domain|en|Matsumae Domain|WD=Q18669712|lt=Matsumae Domain}}, {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}}. In the 4th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}, the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * [[ja:徳山大神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11489606}} [[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} [[Category:Buildings in Matsumae Town, Hokkaido]] {{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}}, {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu}}, they built a small shrine enshrining the {{ill|Cannabis (drug)|en|Cannabis (drug)|WD=Q2845|lt=Osema}} (talisman) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1582|en|1582|WD=Q6719|lt=1582}}), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Shōō}} the lord of the {{ill|Matsumae Domain|en|Matsumae Domain|WD=Q18669712|lt=Matsumae Domain}}, {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}}. In the 4th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}, the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki 拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|41|26|05|N|140|06|38|E}}}} | 所在地 = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}} {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}} {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} 字{{ill|Shinmei|ja|神明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei|1=Shinmei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590276}}66 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|41|26|05|N|140|06|38|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神 = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zō}} | 例祭 = {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|WD=Q2894|lt=September 17}} | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District}}, {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Akita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu}}, they built a small shrine enshrining the {{ill|Cannabis (drug)|en|Cannabis (drug)|WD=Q2845|lt=Osema}} (talisman) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1582|en|1582|WD=Q6719|lt=1582}}), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Shōō}} the lord of the {{ill|Matsumae Domain|en|Matsumae Domain|WD=Q18669712|lt=Matsumae Domain}}, {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}}. In the 4th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}, the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * [[ja:徳山大神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11489606}} [[Category: in the following year (the 53rd year of Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (【associated with】 {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} [[Category:Buildings in Matsumae Town, Hokkaido]] 拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|41|26|05|N|140|06|38|E}}}} | 所在地 = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District|1=Matsumae District, Hokkaido}} {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} 字{{ill|Shinmei|ja|神明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei|1=Shinmei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590276}}66 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|41|26|05|N|140|06|38|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神 = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} | 社格 = 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zō|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭 = {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|WD=Q2894|lt=September 17|1=September 17}} | 神事 = {{ill|Matsumae Kagura|ja|松前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Kagura|qq=}}
{{ill|Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja|鎮釜湯立神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkama Yudate Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=26|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38|label={{ill|Tokuyama Daijingu|ja|徳山大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Daijingu|qq=}}|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | image = File:Tokuyama daijingu.jpg | location = {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364|1=Matsumae, Hokkaido}} | coordinates = {{Coord|41.434722|N|140.110556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724|1=Toyouke-hime}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Tokuyama Daijingu''' (徳山大神宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|en|Matsumae District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1145821|lt=Matsumae District|1=Matsumae District, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1307271}} at Shinmei 66. Its enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, which are the primary deities, although an additional 46 deities are also enshrined (as described below). The former shrine Ranking under the modern system was that of a Gōsha, and it is called the "Ichinomiya of Oshima" (渡島一宮). == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The primary enshrined deities are {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, and additionally 46 other deities are enshrined together (see below). == History == Although the exact date of establishment is unknown, it is伝えられている that in the past, fishermen from {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|1=Q11256953|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256953}} and {{ill|Tsugaru|ja|津軽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 津軽地方|1=Tsugaru region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556401}} would travel here every spring for fishing and return in autumn. To escape dangers posed by the {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu|1=Ainu people}}, they built a small shrine enshrining the {{ill|Cannabis (drug)|en|Cannabis (drug)|WD=Q2845|lt=Osema|1=Cannabis (drug)}} (talisman) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} in {{ill|Karatsunai Town|ja|唐津内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatsunai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, calling it "Ise-dō." In the 10th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1582|en|1582|WD=Q6719|lt=1582|1=1582}}), the feudal lord {{ill|Kaisaki Norihiro|ja|蛎崎（松前）季広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisaki Norihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} relocated his seat to Kuramachi (present-day Fukuyama in Matsumae Town, 松前町字福山) and renamed the shrine as "Shinmei-sha" in reverence. In the first year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Shōō|1=Jōō (Edo period)}} the lord of the {{ill|Matsumae Domain|en|Matsumae Domain|WD=Q18669712|lt=Matsumae Domain|1=Matsumae Domain}}, {{ill|Matsumae Takahiro|ja|松前高広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takahiro|1=Matsumae Takahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514302}}, built a new shrine building at the present location, relocating the shrine and officially receiving the sacred seal ({{ill|Shinji|ja|神璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji|1=Shenxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5371535}}) from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. In the 4th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} in November, it was Ranked as a mura-sha, and in December of the 8th year of Meiji the shrine was renamed "Tokuyama Daijingu," and in the following year (the 9th year of Meiji) it was promoted to a gōsha. In February of the 4th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} it was designated as a Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine. On March 5 of the 46th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}, the main shrine building was designated as a tangible cultural property of Hokkaido, and it was repaired in the 52nd year of Showa; concurrently, the worship hall was rebuilt in the Shinmei style, with Completion tier 1]]) * Showa). == Sub-shrines == Within the shrine precincts is a subsidiary shrine: Haguro Shrine (【associated with】 {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}). == Cultural Properties == * Main shrine building (designated by Hokkaido) * Ridge plaque (designated by Hokkaido) (36 units) * Torii (designated by Hokkaido) == 合祀神 == Because 47 nearby shrines were merged, the following 46 deities are enshrined together (since some shrines enshrined the same deity, the same deity appears multiple times): {{columns-list|colwidth=20em| * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|譽田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki
